app.phasesHint: Screens (boot → lobby → game → game over) arrive in phases.

start.description: A strategic card game about the real grind of software development. Bugs, surprise events, rivals' attacks — beat it all and release first.
start.lobbyNote: Player limit and match modes are configured in the lobby — no need to recreate anything.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: An unexpected error occurred. Try reloading the page.

rules.foot: Match modes (hand limit, Fast Release, release condition, AI count) are chosen before the match — details will appear in the game creation window.

sections.title: Goal
body: Be the first to place three different Release cards (Frontend, Backend, Database) in your release zone, repelling all opponents' attacks — or be the last player standing.

sections.title: Setup
body: Two decks by card back colour: green — main, purple — events.
Each player: 1 Debugger card + 4 random cards (5 in hand total).
AI and Error 503 cards are not allowed at start — swap them from your hand.
Shuffle both decks and place them face down.

sections.title: Player turn
sections.note: action / draw / action / end of turn
body: Before and after drawing you may play any number of cards (including 0).
Draw — exactly one card from the top of the main deck (mandatory).
End of turn is declared with the word "PUSH"; play passes clockwise.

sections.title: Releasing cards
body: Release zone — place for Release cards; one of each type.
Only one release per turn; when played, discard 1 card from hand.
Opponents may immediately respond to a fresh release with a lightning attack (Bug, Out of Memory, Legacy Code, Security Bug).
Code Review is played together with Release and makes it immune to these attacks (even with Sudo). Cannot be applied to an already-placed release.

sections.title: Attack and defence
body: Attack cards (lightning) are played instantly — targeting a release or a hand.
Defence: Cancel (Hotfix, Rubber Ducky, PR Approved, Rollback) and Unicorn (Not a Bug, Works on my Machine).
Sudo enhances cards with the sudo effect; Cancel does not work against the enhancement, Unicorn does.
DDoS — the only attack against a protected release and Monitoring.

sections.title: Trigger cards
body: AI: when drawn, reveal to all and play a random effect from the events deck.
Error 503: when drawn, reveal to all and neutralise (Debugger, Monitoring, or sacrifice a release) — otherwise you're eliminated.

sections.title: End of game
body: The game ends when a player simultaneously has three different Release cards in their release zone — or when only one player remains.

lobby.kickedMessage: You were removed from the game.
lobby.startHint: Everyone must be ready to start.
lobby.title: Lobby
lobby.subtitle: Waiting for players…
lobby.disbandConfirm: The lobby will be closed and all connected players will be disconnected. This cannot be undone.
